channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: Second California real estate agent charged with price gouging victims
item.description: California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced that charges have been filed against another realtor for price gouging victims of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Disturbing messages seen in empty lot in downtown Los Angeles are not nefarious: LAPD
item.description: A patch of undeveloped land in downtown Los Angeles is going viral on social media for some distressing messages seen from above, but police have confirmed that they found no illicit activity in the area.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: Largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in US history hits Kansas, officials say
item.description: The Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) says Kansas is facing the largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in American history.
item.dc:creator: Colter Robinson, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked a push from President Donald Trump to pause federal funding while his administration conducts an across-the-board ideological review to uproot progressive initiatives. The Trump administration plan plunged the U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A$AP Rocky's ex-friend and accuser says tensions were high in moments before alleged shooting
item.description: A$AP Rocky 's accuser, former friend and the key witness testified Tuesday that their relationship had long been tense, and came to a head in the days before Rocky is alleged to have fired a handgun at him.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What Trump's funding freeze could mean for universities, nonprofits and more
item.description: A White House directive to pause federal grants and loans raised uncertainty and fears among many who depend on federal aid before a judge intervened Tuesday to temporarily block it from taking effect.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US education agency investigating Denver schools over all-gender bathroom
item.description: The U.S. Education Department said it is investigating Denver Public Schools for alleged discrimination after the district converted a girl’s restroom on the second floor of East High School into an all-gender restroom while leaving the other bathroom exclusively for male students.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Study says climate change made conditions that fed California wildfires more likely, more intense
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Human-caused climate change increased the likelihood and intensity of the hot, dry and windy conditions that fanned the flames of the recent devastating Southern California wildfires, a scientific study found.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump offers all federal workers a buyout with 7 months' pay in effort to shrink size of government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The White House on Tuesday began offering buyouts worth seven months of salary to all federal employees who opt to leave their jobs by Feb. 6 — part of President Donald Trump's unprecedented overhaul of the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Karoline Leavitt, the youngest White House press secretary, makes her debut in the briefing room
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt made her debut in the briefing room on Tuesday and promised to speak frequently with the news media and open up the briefing room to podcasters and social media influencers.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California water regulators deny Trump's claim that US military 'turned on the water' in the state
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California water officials said this week there's no truth to President Donald Trump's assertion that the U.S. military has entered California and “turned on the water.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Indiana man pardoned by Trump is fatally shot during a traffic stop
item.description: RENSSELAER, Ind. (AP) — An Indiana man was fatally shot by a deputy during a traffic stop just days after he was pardoned by President Donald Trump for a misdemeanor related to the 2021 U.S. Capitol riot.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Proposal would require parents to provide immigration status of students
item.description: “OKCPS does not, nor do we have plans to, collect the immigration status of our students or their families,” she said.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Shooting at Indiana grocery store leaves 3 dead, including the suspect, and 2 officers wounded
item.description: When police “engaged” the shooting suspect outside the store, two officers were wounded and the suspect was fatally shot, according to the Elkhart Police Department.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Gary Peters will not run for reelection, opening key Senate seat
item.description: The 66-year-old’s unexpected decision Tuesday to step aside after just two terms comes as a surprise.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Additional Texas National Guard troops arrive at border
item.description: Over 400 Texas National Guard troops and equipment began arriving in the Rio Grande Valley on Monday evening to assist with border security.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana grocery store shooting leaves 3 dead, including suspect
item.description: A shooting at a grocery store in Elkhart, Indiana, on Monday left three people dead, including the shooter, and two officers injured.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs order that likely sets in motion a future ban on transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday directing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops, likely setting in motion a future ban on their military service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shooting at Indiana store leaves 3 dead, including the suspect, and 2 officers wounded
item.description: ELKHART, Ind. (AP) — A shooting at a grocery store in northern Indiana on Monday left three people dead, including the suspected shooter, and two police officers wounded, officials said. Police received a 911 call at about 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jan. 6 rioter recently pardoned by Trump shot, killed by sheriff's deputy
item.description: An Indiana man recently pardoned by President Donald Trump for storming the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, is dead after being shot by a sheriff's deputy while allegedly resisting arrest.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Person seen firing gun into air amid crowd of Philadelphia Eagles fans; police investigating
item.description: Video of the alleged incident, shared to X late on Sunday, appeared to show a large crowd celebrating the Eagles' win when a person raised the gun into the air.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Haitian migrants in Springfield, Ohio, turn to faith amid deportation fears
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ohio (AP) — At the end of his Sunday service, the pastor of the First Haitian Evangelical Church of Springfield, Ohio, asked ushers and musicians to form a circle around him as he knelt in prayer, flanked by the flags of Haiti and the United States.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Chiefs get more Mahomes magic and advance to 3rd straight Super Bowl, beating the Bills 32-29
item.description: Patrick Mahomes threw for 245 yards and a touchdown, ran through the Buffalo defense for two more scores, and relied on his defense at key moments to help the Kansas City Chiefs beat the Bills 32-29 on Sunday night and reach the Super Bowl for the third consecutive season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio State celebrates national championship run amid change and farewells
item.description: Fans braved 30-degree temperatures to welcome home a team that beat five top-10 rivals on its way to the title, including Oregon in the Rose Bowl, Texas in the Cotton Bowl and Notre Dame in the College Football Playoff championship game.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE KRAFT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Researchers studying whether medical cannabis could help late-stage dementia patients
item.description: As research continues, those involved say this would be a game changer, especially for patients with very limited options.
item.dc:creator: Josh Shortt, ABC 4

item.title: Mel Gibson’s ‘Flight Risk’ is No. 1 at box office, ‘The Brutalist’ expands
item.description: Critics lambasted it and audiences didn’t grade it much better. But despite the turbulence, Mel Gibson’s “Flight Risk” managed to open No. 1 at the box office with a modest $12 million, according to studio estimates Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Freighter on the move after it was freed from ice on frozen Lake Erie
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A freighter was on its way to Canada on Sunday after it was freed from ice that trapped it in a frozen Lake Erie for days, the U.S. Coast Guard said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Black History Month movie celebration at AMC Theatres
item.description: The movie theater chain will bring back seven fan-favorite films from the past year for the month of February. Tickets for each show are already available to purchase online.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, Fox 5

item.title: Jets agree to terms with Broncos executive Darren Mougey to be their general manager
item.description: The New York Jets' lengthy searches are over, and they're pairing a first-time general manager with a first-time head coach to try to turn things around.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK JR., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At Paris Fashion Week, Hermes menswear has a dash of jockey-style boldness
item.description: Hermes illuminated a gloomy Paris afternoon with its fall men’s collection Saturday, as Veronique Nichanian turned to the racetrack for inspiration.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former NBA star marks two years of his cannabis shop in St. Louis
item.description: There was celebration and appreciation in downtown St. Louis Friday as former NBA star Al Harrington marked the two-year anniversary of Viola Cannabis Dispensary.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, ABC 4

item.title: 4 tips for launching a side hustle at any age
item.description: Either start your own business or join an existing side-hustle platform that matches people with opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, Fox 5

item.title: Ozempic and similar weight loss drugs may lower risk of 42 health conditions, but also pose risks
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Ozempic in 2021 for weight loss treatment ushered in a new era for the class of drugs called glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists, or GLP-1.
item.dc:creator: Ziyad Al-Aly Clinical Epidemiologist, Washington University in St. Louis, Fox 5
